meta.description: High-signal items only: contracts, earnings, funding, IPO signals, defense awards, acquisitions. urgency: immediate=act now, same_day=review today, watch=monitor

immediate.title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch on 34th SpaceX Resupply Mission to Station
immediate.summary: The 34th SpaceX commercial resupply mission under contract with NASA is headed to the International Space Station with new scientific experiments after lifting off at 6:05 p.m. EDT Friday on a Falcon 9 rocket from Space Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida. The SpaceX spacecraft, loaded with nearly 6,500 pounds []

immediate.title: Space Force Awards Northrop Grumman $398 Million For Enhanced PTS-P
immediate.summary: The Space Forces Space Systems Command (SSC) has awarded Northrop Grumman [NOC] a $398 million firm-fixed price contract to develop and build an Enhanced Protected Tactical Satellite Communications-Prototype (PTS-P) for [] The post Space Force Awards Northrop Grumman $398 Million For Enhanced PTS-P appeared first on Via Satellite .

immediate.title: Space Force awards Northrop Grumman $398 million satellite contract
immediate.summary: The company will build a communications satellite to test protected communications technologies for contested environments

immediate.title: Air Force Changes Chaplains’ Role in Religious Waiver Requests
immediate.summary: The Air Force recently released an updated policy implementing the Pentagon’s new guidance on troops’ religious accommodation requests. The new policy emphasizes the role commanders play in reviewing such requests, limiting the role of chaplains and eliminating special boards meant to advise leaders. The post Air Force Changes Chaplains’ Role in Religious Waiver Requests appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: New Air-to-Air Missile Photographed in Testing at Eglin
immediate.summary: The military’s secretive new air-to-air missile has been publicly photographed for the first time during testing. The AIM-260 Joint Advanced Tactical Missile, or JATM, was photographed being carried by an F/A-18F Super Hornet on May 13, shortly after taking off from Eglin Air Force Base in Florida. The post New Air-to-Air Missile Photographed in Testing at Eglin appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Skynopy to Support U-Space Missions With Ground Station Services
immediate.summary: Skynopy has won a new contract from U-Space, a Toulouse-based next-generation small satellite manufacturer. Skynopy will be U-Space’s ground station service provider for the operation of its two satellites in [] The post Skynopy to Support U-Space Missions With Ground Station Services appeared first on Via Satellite .

immediate.title: Air Force Reservists Save 11 Plane Crash Survivors at Sea
immediate.summary: Members of the Air Force Reserve’s 920th Rescue Wing helped save 11 airplane crash survivors off the coast of Florida on May 12. The Reserve Airmen were flying an HC-130J Combat King II and an HH-60W Jolly Green II on a routine training flight when a Coast Guard call diverted their mission. The post Air Force Reservists Save 11 Plane Crash Survivors at Sea appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Johnson Photographers Honored for Award-Winning Portraits
immediate.summary: Three photographers at NASA’s Johnson Space Center who inspire the world through visual storytelling earned top honors in the portrait category at the 2025 NASA Imagery Experts Program Annual Awards. “Congratulations to all three on this impressive achievement and for capturing such breathtaking imagery,” said Johnson Director Vanessa Wyche. “Their work represents the collaboration, precision, []

immediate.title: Two Navy EA-18G Growlers Collide at Air Force Base Air Show
immediate.summary: Two Navy EA-18G Growlers collided midair on May 17 during an air show at Mountain Home Air Force Base, Idaho. All four crew members ejected safely, and there were no fatalities. The post Two Navy EA-18G Growlers Collide at Air Force Base Air Show appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: What time is SpaceX's Starship V3 launch on May 20? (Starship Flight 12 timeline)
immediate.summary: SpaceX will launch its first Starship V3 vehicle on the Flight 12 test flight on Wednesday (May 20).

immediate.title: Watch Vega C rocket launch European-Chinese space weather satellite to orbit tonight
immediate.summary: A Vega C rocket will launch the European-Chinese SMILE space weather mission to orbit tonight (May 18), and you can watch the action live.

immediate.title: US Air Force looks to convert offshore oil rigs into rocket recovery platforms
immediate.summary: An Air Force plan calls for old oil platforms to become Sea-based Recovery Stations for the U.S. Space Force and private spaceflight companies.

immediate.title: SpaceX Starship Flight 12 launch updates: 1st-ever Starship V3 test gets new launch date
immediate.summary: SpaceX is targeting a Wednesday, May 20, launch for its newest Starship design, the Starship V3 megarocket.

immediate.title: Inside Golden Dome’s push to court commercial tech firms and investors
immediate.summary: Behind the classified architecture is a struggle over affordability, industrial scale and whether commercial space economics can work for national defense

immediate.title: U-Space Selects Skynopy to Support Multiple Missions
immediate.summary: Skynopy’s ground-station-as-a-service model is quickly gaining traction. The post U-Space Selects Skynopy to Support Multiple Missions appeared first on Payload .

immediate.title: Giant radio telescope sees Artemis 2 astronauts on Orion flying around the moon. 'There are 4 people in those pixels.'
immediate.summary: A giant radio telescope in West Virginia tracked Artemis 2's Orion spacecraft around the moon for five days, gathering precise observations on its movements during the historic mission.

immediate.title: York Space defends growth strategy as SDA programs are reshaped
immediate.summary: Company executives acknowledge ‘investor confusion’ as Space Force reorganizes Yorks primary customer, the Space Development Agency

immediate.title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 210 — ESCAPADES at Mars
immediate.summary: On Episode 210 of This Week In Space, Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik talk with Dr. Robert Lillis of the Mars ESCAPADE mission.

immediate.title: SpaceX launches CRS-34 cargo mission to ISS
immediate.summary: A Falcon 9 launched a Dragon cargo spacecraft May 15 carrying nearly 3,000 kilograms of cargo to the International Space Station.

immediate.title: Astrolab Unveils Payloads Flying on FLIP Lunar Mission
immediate.summary: Astrolab is headed to the Moon this year with a rover-load of science payloads. The post Astrolab Unveils Payloads Flying on FLIP Lunar Mission appeared first on Payload .

immediate.title: 'Voidverse' is a seriously weird space fantasy novel from 'The OA's' Damien Ober (interview)
immediate.summary: 'For the atmosphere and world of 'Voidverse' to work, I needed the characters and the prose to meld with the setting.'

immediate.title: Four NASA payloads to fly on Astrolab’s first lunar rover
immediate.summary: Astrolab’s first lunar rover will carry four NASA payloads on a mission planned to launch later this year.

immediate.title: Five companies win DoD’s Drone Dominance small drone ‘Lethality Prize Challenge’
immediate.summary: [The] Lethality Challenge selection gives us a rail-locked pathway to thousands or tens of thousands of unit orders for this product, which has absolutely changed the caliber of discussion we’re having with investors, suppliers, other customers/partners, etc, drone-maker Bravos Kevin Landtroop told Breaking Defense.

immediate.title: NASA’s MAVEN Makes 1st Discovery of Atmospheric Effect at Mars
immediate.summary: In December 2023, scientists looking at Mars data stumbled across something completely unexpected — observations of an atmospheric effect never before seen in the Red Planet’s atmosphere. Using instruments aboard NASA’s MAVEN (Mars Atmosphere and Volatile Evolution) mission, scientists identified a phenomenon known to occur in Earth’s magnetosphere, where charged particles are squeezed like toothpaste […]

immediate.title: NASA Selects Next Class of Space Health Postdoctoral Fellows
immediate.summary: The NASA-funded Translational Research Institute for Space Health (TRISH) has selected two early‑career scientists for its next class of postdoctoral fellows. The new fellows will begin their projects in May, focusing on space food systems and astronaut eye health. The TRISH Postdoctoral Fellowship Program supports independent research that advances biomedical, behavioral, and technological approaches relevant []

same_day.title: Tomorrow.io adds $35 million to DeepSky funding round
same_day.summary: Weather intelligence provider Tomorrow.io has added $35 million to its latest funding round, bringing the total to $210 million to accelerate development of a next-generation constellation for gathering atmospheric data.

same_day.title: House bill restores funding for TraCSS
same_day.summary: A House appropriations bill would reverse plans by the administration to stop development of a civil space traffic management system.

same_day.title: SpaceX Starlink and other satellite megaconstellations are creating an 'unregulated geoengineering experiment', scientists say
same_day.summary: The space industry's plans to launch millions of satellites worry scientists as the rise in high-altitude air pollution threatens to alter Earth's climate.

same_day.title: Zenk Space raises $26 million, targets June debut launch
same_day.summary: China’s Zenk Space has secured 180 million yuan ahead of the planned June debut of its Zhihang-1 kerolox rocket, the companys first orbital launch attempt.

same_day.title: Starlink Mobile challengers back proposed telco-led D2D joint venture
same_day.summary: A planned direct-to-device joint venture between ATT, T-Mobile and Verizon has divided operators behind the satellites needed to connect ordinary smartphones from space.

same_day.title: Beacon of Light
same_day.summary: The heart of galaxy M77 shines brightly in this May 7, 2026, image from NASAs James Webb Space Telescope. The intense glow is due to gas being pulled by the strong gravity of the central black hole into a tight and rapid orbit around it. The motion of the gas causes it to heat up, []

same_day.title: This amazing NASA video shows the exact moment the Artemis 2 Orion capsule broke free of its service module, and we can't stop watching it
same_day.summary: A new NASA video shows the Artemis 2 Orion capsule's separation from its service module just before the spacecraft slammed into Earth's atmosphere on April 10, 2026.

same_day.title: These Apollo 'UFO' images have been public for decades. So why are people talking about them now?
same_day.summary: Much of the UFO imagery that the Pentagon released last week was new, but we've known for decades about the odd things that the Apollo astronauts saw on and around the moon.

same_day.title: SpaceX Dragon capsule delivers science and supplies to space station
same_day.summary: A SpaceX Dragon cargo spacecraft delivered 6,500 pounds of science and supplies to the International Space Station on Sunday, May 17, 2026.

same_day.title: NASA's Psyche spacecraft is flying to a metal asteroid. So, why did it just visit Mars?
same_day.summary: NASA's Psyche flyby of Mars highlights how gravity assists help spacecraft gain speed, conserve fuel and travel across the solar system.

watch.title: Aeralis Collapses, Ending Hopes For a British Designed Advanced Jet Trainer
watch.summary: Aeralis Limited was working on a modular advanced jet trainer for the Royal Air Force, with hopes to equip the Red Arrows to replace the Hawk T1. Following the US and Israeli war with Iran alongside delays to the Defence Investment Plan (DIP), Aeralis Limited has become another British defence company to slip into administration []
